Characters attempting t0 use Stealth are detected by the opponent" s Perception Skill in a Opposed Test: Perception Cnecks give the gamemaster good way to keep story moving and t0 tell the players how much their char acters see and when they see it. Be careful not t0 overuse Perception Checks, however; Or they will bog down the flow of play: As with everything else, resort t0 game mechanics only when Ine outcome is uncertain_

PILOTING" The Piloting Subskills cover the operation of the various vehicles of the thirty first century; from simple ground cars to the mighty BattleMech; Piloting Skill Checks are required when character attempts difficult or dangerous maneuver: Failure indicates the character has lost control of the vehicle and will crash (or fall in the case of BattleMechs). Piloting Skill can also be used t0 quickly resolve vehiclc chases_ Tne pilot of each vehicle makes an Opposed Piloting Test: If the vehicles have the same maximum speed (flanking for vehicles or running for `Mechs), only apply the usual modi- fiers If one vehicle is faster than the other, the slower vehicle pilot must add modifier to his target number equal t0 the amount of kph the other vehicle' maximum speed exceeds his own divided by 10 (round down) For example ground car with flanking speed kph being chased light BattleMech with running speed 0f 119 kph. The ground car is slower; so the TN for its Piloting Roll is raised by +3 ([119 86] 10 3.31. If the chasing vehicle has the higher Margin of Success_ the fleeing vehicle caught: If the fleeing vehicle has higher MoS_ It  escapes If neither  pilot succeeded at their Piloting Rolls both vehicles must roll again_ but the faster vehicle gains Modifier to Its TN:

PROTOCOL" character with Protocol Skill has the social skills to inter- act with others professional and courteous manner ad iS well versed in etiquette rules and social rituals specific to the particular regional Protocol Subskill ne has. Protocol is crucia for diplomats and nobility: but IS also useful for any character who comes in contact with socicty upper echelons
Piloting Subskills The subskills are listed on (ne Piloting Subskills Table in related groups of vehicles whose controls are similar enough that character trained in one will nave reasonable chance of piloting the others. When character pilots related vehicle that he has no skill with, use the Untrained Skill Use rules for standard skills rather than difficult skills_ and apply tne charac ter"$ skill bonus in the related skill rather than the Untrained Use Modifiers for Attributes
QUICKDRAW character with the Quickdraw Skill can draw small weapon (gamemaster- discretion) and use it before another individual can react. Tne character must make Quickdra Skill Check - t0 deter mne he can get snol Or throw off before the enemy has chance t0 react: If successful, the character gets one attack with his quickdrawn weapon before the actual start of the first combat turn.
